proposals.label: Procedure Fetish in AI Governance
proposals.description:
A situation concept that describes the tendency of regulatory bodies to prioritize procedural compliance over substantive safety outcomes.
Encompasses: The risk of 'runaway bureaucracy' where process becomes the goal.
Excludes: Effective, outcome-based safety regulation.
proposals.rationale: This concept describes a specific, recurring failure mode in AI governance that is distinct from general regulatory inertia, appearing in multiple policy critiques.

proposals.label: Introspection as AI Capability
proposals.description:
A situation concept that refers to the degree to which an AI system can monitor and report on its own internal states or reasoning processes.
Encompasses: Self-reflection, internal state monitoring, and recursive self-improvement potential.
Excludes: General alignment or capability testing.
proposals.rationale: Introspection is a distinct, measurable capability debated across all three POVs with divergent interpretations regarding its utility and risk.

proposals.label: Representational Risk in Self-Supervised Learning
proposals.description:
A Belief within safetyist discourse that defects encoded in the learned geometry of a model during training are fundamentally unfixable post-hoc.
Encompasses: Inherent biases or structural flaws in latent space that persist despite fine-tuning.
Excludes: Surface-level output biases or alignment failures.
proposals.rationale: This concept identifies a specific technical risk that necessitates upstream liability and design changes, distinct from standard alignment failures.
